'''Josh Mantellato''' (born 21 April 1987) is a former [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played on the {{rlp|wg}} for [[The Entrance Tigers]] in the Denton Engineering Cup. He previously played for the [[Wyong Roos]] in the [[Central Coast Division Rugby League]] competition, [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
[[File:Joshua Mantellato 2013 RLWC.jpg|thumb|left|Mantellato playing for [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].]]
Mantellato was born in [[Gosford]], [[New South Wales]], Australia and is of [[Italians|Italian]] descent.

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Wyong Roos]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Up until 2011, Mantellato played for the [[Wyong Roos]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]].

===2010===
In 2010, Mantellato made his international debut for [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]].

===2012===
In 2012, Mantellato joined the [[Newcastle Knights]]' [[NSW Cup]] team, finishing the season as the competition's highest point-scorer, with 306 points. On 12 September 2012, he was named on the wing in the 2012 [[NSW Cup]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=63751&title=2012-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement|title=2012 NSWRL.com.au VB NSW Cup Team of the Year - Announcement|access-date=16 June 2015|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121010140055/http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=63751&title=2012-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement|archive-date=10 October 2012 }}</ref> He then signed a one-year contract with the Newcastle outfit.<ref>{{cite web|last=Dillon|first=Robert|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/1544344/mantellatos-knights-debut/?cs=306|title=Mantellato's Knights debut | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2013-06-02|access-date=2015-06-18}}</ref>

===2013===
In round 12 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Mantellato made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Newcastle against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Mantellato-to-debut/76050|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140201164835/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Mantellato-to-debut/76050|archive-date=1 February 2014 |title=Mantellato to debut – Newcastle Knights }}</ref> He scored a try and kicked three goals on debut.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2013/round-12/south-sydney-vs-newcastle/summary.html|title=NRL 2013 – Round 12|publisher=Rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=2015-06-18}}</ref> On 20 August 2013, he re-signed with the Novocastrians on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Mantellato-resigns-for-2014/83274|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131206004323/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Mantellato-resigns-for-2014/83274|archive-date=6 December 2013 |title=Mantellato re-signs for 2014 – Newcastle Knights }}</ref> On 22 September 2013, he was again named on the wing in the 2013 [[NSW Cup]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=84880&title=2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement|title=2013 NSWRL.com.au VB NSW Cup Team of the Year - Announcement|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140111155627/http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=84880&title=2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement|archive-date=11 January 2014 }}</ref> During 2013, he featured in Italy's successful [[2013 Rugby League World Cup qualifying]] tournament and was selected in the 24-man squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] campaign.<ref>{{cite web|last=Leeson|first=Josh|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/476276/wyong-fullback-josh-mantellato-in-line-for-italian-selection/|title=Wyong fullback Josh Mantellato in line for Italian selection | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2011-09-22|access-date=2015-06-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1117/italy-name-squad-for-rlwc|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130927082325/http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1117/italy-name-squad-for-rlwc|url-status=usurped|archive-date=27 September 2013|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013|publisher=Rlwc2013.com|access-date=2015-06-18}}</ref> On 19 October 2013, he kicked the winning field goal in Italy's 15-14 warm up game win over [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/league/italy-pull-off-shock-victory-over-england-in-rugby-league-world-cup-warm-up-match/story-e6frf3ou-1226743280777|title=Italy pull-off shock victory over England in Rugby League World Cup warm-up match|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-06-18|date=20 October 2013}}</ref>

===2014===
On 21 September 2014, Mantellato was named on the wing in the 2014 [[NSW Cup]] Team of the Year for the third year in a row.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/09/21/_2014_vb_nsw_cup_team_of_the_year.html|title=2014 VB NSW Cup Team of the Year|publisher=NSWRL.com.au|access-date=2015-06-18|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150708221216/http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/09/21/_2014_vb_nsw_cup_team_of_the_year.html|archive-date=8 July 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2015===
On 11 August 2014, Mantellato signed a three-year contract with [[Super League]] club, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], starting in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/08/11/mantellato_to_join_hull_kr.html|title=Mantellato to join Hull KR – Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|date=11 August 2014|access-date=2015-06-18}}</ref> He made his Super League debut in round 1 against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He kicked off his Super League points tally by kicking five goals in their 30-40 first round defeat. He scored his first two Super League tries against the [[Warrington Wolves]] in round 3.

===2017===
In January, Mantellato returned to Australia to play for [[Sydney Roosters]] feeder club, Wyong Roos on a one-year contract following the relegation of Hull Kingston Rovers to the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.5thtackle.com/josh-mantellato-joins-sydney-roosters-feeder-club/|title=Josh Mantellato Joins Sydney Roosters Feeder Club|publisher=5th Tackle|date=18 January 2017|access-date=2017-01-23}}</ref>

===2018===
Mantellato spent 2018 playing for Wyong in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]].  At the end of the season it was announced that Wyong would not be competing in the competition in 2019 as the [[Sydney Roosters]] ended their agreement for Wyong to be their feeder side instead choosing foundation club [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]] as the new feeder side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/11/05/rip-taylor-returns-to-wentworthville/|title=Rip Taylor returns to Wentworthville|website=NSWRL|date=5 November 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/04/12/josh-mantellatos-epic-adventure/|title=Josh Mantellato's Epic Adventure|website=NSWRL|date=12 April 2018}}</ref>

===2021===
After exiting the Wyong Roos, Mantellato joined the [[Entrance Tigers]] for Season 2021. At the advice of medical professionals, Mantellato would go on to retire after sustaining a throat fracture.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/life-after-league-with-josh-mantellato-representing-italy-time-at-hull-kr-growing-the-game/ | title=Life after League with Josh Mantellato: Representing Italy, time at Hull KR & growing the game | date=26 July 2021 }}</ref>
